title: Bridge Essay: Modeling the effects of motivation on choice and learning in the basal ganglia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Knowledge/Gnosis** (Phase 14) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Knowledge/Gnosis to mechanistic neuroscience through Modeling, the, effects, of, motivation.

**Modeling the effects of motivation on choice and learning in the basal ganglia.** — PLoS Comput Biol. PMID: 32453725. DOI: 10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007465. Decision making relies on adequately evaluating the consequences of actions on the basis of past experience and the current physiological state. Akey role in this process is played by the basal ganglia, where neural activity and plasticity are modulated by dopaminergic input from the midbrain. Internal physiological factors, such as hunger, scale signals encoded by dopaminergic neurons and thus they alter the motivation for taking actions and learning.
